questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: The Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act (PWDVA), 2005 was primarily enacted to fulfill India's obligations under which international convention?

options.text: The Beijing Declaration and Platform for Action
Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW)
The Istanbul Convention on preventing and combating violence against women
International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights (ICCPR)

questions.explanation: India is a signatory to the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW). As a signatory, India was required to legislate specifically against domestic violence to recognize it as a human rights violation.
questions.takeaway: PWDVA 2005 is the domestic legislative fulfillment of India's international commitment under CEDAW.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: India, as a signatory to Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination Against Women (CEDAW), was required to legislate specifically against domestic violence, recognizing it as a violation of human rights.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: Which of the following best describes the jurisdictional reach of the Internal Complaints Committee (ICC) under the Sexual Harassment of Women at Workplace (PoSH) Act, 2013?

options.text: The ICC can only exercise jurisdiction over employees working within the same physical office premises as the aggrieved woman.
The ICC of the aggrieved woman's workplace is enabled to exercise jurisdiction over an employee even if they belong to a different workplace.
The ICC must transfer the case to the Local Committee (LC) if the respondent is not an employee of the same organization.
The ICC's jurisdiction is limited to permanent employees and does not extend to contractual staff from other agencies.

questions.explanation: The PoSH Act enables the ICC constituted at the aggrieved woman's workplace to exercise jurisdiction over an employee of a different workplace, ensuring that the victim can seek redressal through her own organization's mechanism regardless of the harasser's employer.
questions.takeaway: The PoSH Act provides for cross-workplace jurisdictional powers to the ICC to protect the aggrieved woman.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: It enables ICC constituted at aggrieved woman's workplace to exercise jurisdiction over an employee of a different workplace.

questions.subject: Polity
questions.prompt: The Protection of Women from Domestic Violence Act (PWDVA), 2005, was enacted to ensure the fundamental rights of women guaranteed under which of the following Articles of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The PWDVA, 2005 aims to ensure the fundamental rights of women guaranteed under Articles 14 (Equality before law), 15 (Prohibition of discrimination), and 21 (Right to life and personal liberty).
questions.takeaway: The PWDVA is rooted in the constitutional guarantees of equality and the right to a dignified life.
questions.evidenceExcerpt: Aimed to ensure the fundamental rights of women guaranteed under Articles 14, 15 and 21.
